title: Add post-deployment Keystone API health check
summary: Adds a reconcileHealthCheck sub-reconciler that performs an HTTP GET to the Keystone /v3 endpoint after reconcileDeployment confirms readiness. Sets a KeystoneAPIReady condition based on the HTTP response. Classifies network errors (timeout, DNS, connection refused, generic) into descriptive condition reasons and requeues rather than returning errors. Includes HTTPDoer interface for test injection, constants in requeue_intervals.go, updated subConditionTypes, comprehensive unit tests (16 test functions), chain-ordering integration test, and complete documentation updates.

review_steps.1_INTENT: Feature CC-0067 adds an HTTP health check sub-reconciler that verifies the Keystone API is actually serving requests after pods report ready. This bridges the gap between infrastructure readiness and service readiness.
review_steps.2_SCOPE: Changes are strictly within scope: new reconcile_healthcheck.go + test, modified keystone_controller.go (struct + chain + subConditionTypes), requeue_intervals.go (constants + test), and docs updates. No scope creep detected.
review_steps.3_VERIFICATION: go vet passes clean. All 16 health check tests pass. All controller tests pass. golangci-lint reports 1 errcheck finding in new code (resp.Body.Close).
review_steps.4_PATTERNS: Sub-reconciler follows established condition contract pattern with ObservedGeneration. HTTPDoer interface is minimal. Constants co-located in requeue_intervals.go. Test file uses NewWithT instead of codebase-standard NewGomegaWithT.
review_steps.5_TESTS: Comprehensive: healthy 200, unhealthy 500/503, empty endpoint, timeout, DNS error, connection refused, generic error, ObservedGeneration tracking, response body close (success + error), HTTPDoer injection, chain-ordering integration, aggregate-ready inclusion. 16 test functions in healthcheck test + 4 new tests in controller test.
review_steps.6_SECURITY: No security concerns. HTTP client uses bounded context timeout. No secrets in health check path. No user-controlled input beyond Status.Endpoint (set by reconciler, not user).
review_steps.7_COMPLEXITY: Minimal and appropriate. classifyHealthCheckError is a clean pure function. reconcileHealthCheck follows the established sub-reconciler pattern exactly.
review_steps.8_COMPLETENESS: All acceptance criteria met. All 10 tasks marked done. Docs updated with condition type, sub-reconciler section, ASCII flow diagram, CRD example, test file listing, and directory tree.

security_checklist.item: No secrets in health check path

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
Network errors requeue, not returned as errors

fail_fast_checklist.item: Empty endpoint detected early with requeue
All network errors classified and requeued

defensive_checklist.item: HTTP response body always closed via defer
All error paths set condition before requeue

issues_found.description: golangci-lint errcheck: resp.Body.Close() return value is not checked. This is a lint error in the new code that must be resolved for the build to pass clean.
issues_found.fix: Change `defer resp.Body.Close()` to `defer func() { _ = resp.Body.Close() }()` to explicitly discard the error, satisfying errcheck.
issues_found.evidence:
defer resp.Body.Close()

golangci-lint output: operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_healthcheck.go:70:23: Error return value of `resp.Body.Close` is not checked (errcheck)

issues_found.description: reconcile_healthcheck_test.go uses NewWithT(t) (15 occurrences) while all other test files in the same package use NewGomegaWithT(t) (235 occurrences). Both are functionally identical (NewGomegaWithT is an alias), but the codebase convention is NewGomegaWithT per the established 'Gomega test assertions with NewGomegaWithT' pattern.
issues_found.fix: Replace all 15 occurrences of `NewWithT(t)` with `NewGomegaWithT(t)` to match the established codebase convention.
issues_found.evidence:
reconcile_healthcheck_test.go:88: g := NewWithT(t)
reconcile_healthcheck_test.go:98: g := NewWithT(t)
... (15 total)

vs. 235 occurrences of NewGomegaWithT(t) across all other test files in the package

suggested_improvements: Consider adding os.ErrDeadlineExceeded to the timeout classification (currently only checks context.DeadlineExceeded). While HTTP client timeouts use context.DeadlineExceeded, socket-level I/O deadline errors use os.ErrDeadlineExceeded. These are not equivalent per errors.Is. Low risk since http.Client with context timeout always produces context.DeadlineExceeded.
next_steps: Fix errcheck lint error: change defer resp.Body.Close() to defer func() { _ = resp.Body.Close() }()
Replace NewWithT with NewGomegaWithT across reconcile_healthcheck_test.go (15 occurrences)
